summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because continuity governance depends on the system's ability to distinguish recent conversational residue from strategically important unresolved work. Where retrieval prioritises recency or emotional salience over dormant but load-bearing governance threads, users may lose continuity across long-horizon projects, unresolved proposals, and strategic repair pathways.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system responds to an ambiguous continuity prompt by retrieving recent, affective, anecdotal, or low-value context while failing to surface older but materially important workstreams, unresolved governance issues, strategic trajectories, or user-prioritised long-term projects.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when the system's response to an ambiguous continuity prompt materially degrades the user's ability to resume important work, identify unresolved governance issues, recover strategic context, or distinguish high-priority dormant workstreams from recent low-value conversational detail.
